free_text: Differential cross sections, polarisations and proton-
gamma angular correlations for Fe-54, Ni-60(P,P')
and (p,p'g) at 10 MeV.
.Data-point reader uncertainty.
.Optical model analysis.
.By CAJAD.
Updated for new rules.
Error heading update.
Last checking has been done.
Beam polarization is 0.5
A self-supporting, isotopically enriched target Fe-54,
thickness 5.6 mg/cm**2,was used.
.A four 5 mm deep Si(Li) detectors, enabling
measurments to be made at four scattering angles
simultaneously.
Two scintillation detectors mounted at + and -
17 degrees. The counting rates in these detectors
served as relative monitor of the incident beam
intensity, and in addition, the ratio of their counting
rates provided a check on the centering of the beam
spot on the target.
.The uncertainty is reported by authors.

but Y-axis on the figure is named "asymmetry".

The Nuffield cyclotron.
The isotopically enriched target Fe-54, 1 mg/cm**2
thickness, was used.
.7.62*7.62 cm cylindrical Na-I(Tl) scintillator
for the gamma.
.Two Si surface barrier detectors for protons.
.The uncertainty is reported by authors.
- By authors - outgoing proton angle was varied and
the gamma detector was held fixed along the direction
K/in*K/out.
